transcript.text: How's it going?
Hi, everyone.
Good.
Are we, I guess we're waiting for a Yes.
Golzar, I appreciate your Halloween-themed background.
You're actually the first person I've seen with that, so I very appreciate it.
Thank you.
That's cute.
Thanks.
It's a Google Meet.
Golzar, you've had it for two weeks now, right?
I remember seeing it last week as well.
I Yeah.
fun.
I made it a couple weeks ago, and Zoom doesn't have fun backgrounds, so I took a screenshot of the Google Meet background and uploaded it to Zoom.
There you go.
Creative.
I'm glad you did.
Hey, Victor.
Victor, how are you?
Hey, everyone.
I'm good.
How are doing?
Good.
Nice to see you.
Good to see you, too.
Good to you, everybody.
Good morning, everybody.
How are you doing?
Good, good, good.
Good.
Thank you.
Thank you for the podcast mention, Victor.
Super, super appreciate Absolutely.
Sure, yeah.
You guys are doing great work.
Happy to give the credit to the people and do the work.
That was very thoughtful of you.
So we really appreciate it.
Awesome.
All right.
Let me share my screen.
Sorry.
I don't know which Windows went.
There we go.
Cool.
So just running through the updates real quick.
We had a bit of a technical issue with code formatting in our pipeline last week.
So that delayed two articles.
It's a pretty small issue, basically, when articles are super cold.

The code kind of escapes that formatting and it gets published as plain text, so it's a simple enough fix that we're addressing this week, so this week we'll have nine articles instead of the usual seven, and then just a quick review of the outstanding issues, etc., we basically created a new artifact for Strapi.ai using the information that you've given us, and we're trying to just sort of see if there's any opportune insertion or any opportunities for that in articles moving forward.
Running over on the issues real quick, I think the main things were the, from last week, there was that issue of the integration page missing their logos in the main landing page.
All I know, you'd mentioned you were taking a look at it.
I don't know if there's any updates.
don't
I don't think so.
We talked about it yesterday.
Do you get any response to your issue, Paul?
No, not yet.
Yeah, but it's a bit of a bottleneck at the moment with the website.
It's like a more pressing issue.
So it will, hopefully we will be able to move on this later this week.
Cool, no worries.
And I guess you kind of answered the next one as well, but I'll check nonetheless.
Is it possible to remove all the comparison pages that we've already published?

I know we discussed removing it after everything is done, but since there are so many of those pages, I was wondering the ones that, especially the strapi versus ones that we've already published, is it possible to remove it just for those pages?
Or is that something you feel we should do after everything is published?
Ask them.
Mainly because those pages are getting good traffic, and we feel like removing that initial big table will certainly boost performance over there.
I'll let Paul answer that question.
So at the moment, we were discussing, like, if it would be worthwhile for us to add a toggle button.

Ideally, it would be great if all the comparison pages are done so we could just remove it completely, because there is, you know, like, engineering overhead from the standpoint of adding a toggle button in Strapi, and then updating our front-end UI to reflect that, and we're just not sure if that would make sense to do because it's easier just to remove that component completely.
Okay.
All right.
I guess the trade-off between how much performance, because the pages are performing well.
It's just that they're slightly ahead of what they were...
Historically drawing in in terms of traffic.
So I just wanted to throw it out there.
Like, if it's not too much of a lift, maybe removing that.
Yeah, so maybe, like, what we could do, like, here's an idea that I have to think out loud.

Maybe if we can't remove it because, like, you know, there's a little bit more work, maybe if we change the order where your content shows up first and that table is just at the bottom, that could be something that's easily done and not going to require a lot of time from Notum or adding any additional features.
And so maybe that could be, like, a good compromise.
I don't know what you think, Victor.
Yeah, that's a good idea for sure.
Let's try to remove it.
And if this takes too much time, let's just put it at the bottom.
But I do want to call out something that I noticed yesterday.
I was on the Strapi versus Contentful page, and I noticed that there was a video that was getting started with Strapi V4.
So it was an embedding of a wrong video.
So I don't know how we missed that one, but that's something that's pretty bad, because if the page is getting traffic, we're just linking to an old video.
So I fixed it for both WordPress and Contentful on the fly yesterday when I saw it, but we really want to make sure it doesn't happen again for other comparative pages.
We'll take a look.
I think from our end, we sort of focused on the content, and I think some of those pages had videos, some of them didn't.
So we'll take another look this week and just make sure, like, all the videos are kind of up to date.
I think the reason we did that is because we assumed the videos were kind of the same, and it was a basic page template, so we just hit the text.
But yeah, we'll take a look again at that and sort of flag those and fix it wherever it needs to be done.
Yeah, let me check real quick, like, if on the other strapi...
Versus Sanity, for instance.
It looks like it's not there.
It's only on Contentful and WordPress, but it's a shame that those two are the ones that are linked from the footer of our site.
They are definitely the ones that get the most juice.
Yeah.

In general, I do think if we have some videos to add to those pages, it's just like for me, I find that if we have pages that are super text heavy, we kind of need to make those pages more digestible by adding some visual, whether it's videos or illustrations.
And so I think for those ones that are getting the most traffic, let's try to see if we can put videos in there.
And I tried for, you know, WordPress, we have content on people migrating from WordPress to Strapi.
And from Contentful, think we, I forgot which one I added, but I think we can tailor, if we have like a migration story, let's put that video in there.
If not, we just do like a blank, you know, whatever, like getting started with strapi.v5.
So, yeah, I think adding videos, I think would be a good use of, if we're going to, if we see like traction with those pages, I think that's a good addition.
Yeah, we'll do a little review and then sort of follow up with Paul and Theo in the channel to figure out which videos to go on, et cetera.
We'll handle that.
Okay, so just a quick performance update.
So, yeah, basically this last bar, by the way, is October, so it's a work in progress.
But obviously we have one more week of measurement to go for October.
And as you can see, we're on pace to match or at least outpace.
September, the top performers for the past week, pretty much the same articles that we're used to seeing.
There is a VS Code Extensions article which popped up, which was pretty good.
And then also the Next.js for SEO article, which I believe is a recent article that we published, I think, two, three weeks back, that showed up as well.
Generally, non-branded impressions are on pace to match September.
In terms of conversion events, again, October looks like it's going to match September.
Obviously, we had that peak in May, and then July kind of came close to it.
But then since then, we've been in this little range over here.
Back in May, of course, this is just a hypothesis, but back in May, we had that huge sort of increase in non-branded search from the bad forum links.
And then maybe the conversion clicks were an unintended side effect from that.
Massive exposure because the number of clicks absolutely went through the roof in those few months.
But generally, we're on pace to mid-September as well.
In terms of cohort performance, week-on-week, all clusters saw higher traffic.
Positions as well are pretty promising.
And then, yeah, the next point is just regarding, this one is just regarding the strapi comparison pages, which we discussed already.
But generally speaking, all clusters seem to be doing well.
there is potential for refreshes, of course, which we're looking at.
But generally, we're trying to balance that with new content, especially the AI content that we have been developing this week.
In terms of LLM traffic, referral traffic is growing steadily.
So for September, we hit a new high.
October looks like it should outpace it.
So generally, things are going quite well there.
I haven't included the scrunch dashboard because we didn't see any changes week-on-week.
I will include it next week since it will be an end-of-the-month kind of a thing.
So...
Generally, we're holding positions based on what we did last week, and there's no major changes in terms of what we're seeing in this group.
Any questions?
Yeah, a question on this one, Vivek.
I'm not seeing the same thing when I go to the Luka dashboard and look at the LLM.
To me, I see a decrease, so I'm not sure how yours is different from mine.
Is it?
Is this what we picked in July?
Okay.
Yes, I'm not sure.
I'm not seeing the same data.
Well, let me try filtering it again.
Maybe this is like some...
Okay, is this what you're seeing?
Something like this one?
Yeah, correct.
Yeah, yeah.
Okay, this is some kind of a weird Luka issue.
I'm not sure why it's doing this.
Basically, this is a Luka dash issue.
We have a lot of filters.
We
of this chart, and sometimes it reacts weirdly.
I'm not sure why this happens.
Well, actually, I'm actually not seeing the same thing.
Sorry, like, let me share my screen so you can see what I'm...
Yeah.
So, yeah, I'm not filtering on anything here, I just...
Okay, so the screenshot I shared was only the ones that growthx has created.
So under URL cohorts, if you filter out non-growthx URLs and integration page non-growthx.
Okay.
That one, yeah, it should.
Okay.
Yes, just overall, like, to me, like, it's, you know, when I look at it holistically, we still have a decrease, I'm thinking, like, we should maybe look into...
Yeah, I just...
Yeah, I feel like the main...
The vocal traffic is going to strap you
.io, as in the home page.
And so, to me, I feel like this is a bigger issue here in terms of overall traffic, and I just want to do some digging.
I'm not seeing any kind of data on scrunch that speaks to that yet.
But that should be growing, right, as the users of LLMs in general is growing.
mean, like, I feel like we should be growing.
I don't know why it's taking so long on Looker, but anyway.
Yeah, on Looker, this chart takes a while to load, but yeah, generally, you are right.
One thing that we're hoping for is maybe the comparison pages kind of boost the performance of that.
We can certainly take a look at the rest of the website just to see, like, if there's any opportunities for that.
But generally speaking, though, the ones that we've been producing, they, you know.
As you saw part the chart, performance is encouraging.
Maybe we can take, we have been trying a few things like just chunking sections and also choosing certain topics.
Maybe we can condense those best practices a bit and see if it's possible to push it to other parts of your website.
That might be one way to sort of mitigate that.
Yeah, I think that would be great.
For LLM, like referral traffic, I think we should include all URLs and use that as a way to identify opportunities.
Okay.
So let do that.
All right.
So, yeah, I think the only other point of discussion was the, was there a renewal?
I think, Mara?
Yeah, I think, correct me if I'm wrong, I think Bridget sent a contract.
I just wanted to confirm that she did, and if you have any questions whatsoever.
Nothing, I'm good.
We're just validating.
Okay.
That's it on our end, so it should be good to go.
Sounds good.
Yeah.
And, yeah, I got a preview of the upcoming product from Marcel last week, so I'm excited to get my hands on that.
You know, what I shared in our last meeting, Mara, is like I want to ensure the team, so we don't become like a bottleneck, or like your engineering team is not a bottleneck.
Like, I want to be excited about self-serving in some cases, and it seems like the new product will allow us to do that.
Yeah.
So, yeah, we're looking forward to getting more information on when this is going to land.
Yep, totally.
I know, I'm sure you mentioned that to Marcel, maybe when you're walking through the product.
I also mentioned it to engineering and our operations team, just to like make it exceedingly obvious that whenever that rolls out, we would love to pilot it with you.
So, I'll keep you posted there, but it is definitely...
Top of mind for us.
And there's visibility like internally, obviously, that there's interest from Strapi.
So I'll keep you posted on the timeline once I have it.
That sounds good.
Yeah.
Cool.
Great.
All right.
Well, thanks so much, everyone.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review content performance, resolve technical issues, and align on next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Pipeline:** Two articles delayed by a code formatting bug will be published this week, bringing the total to nine.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)
  - [**Comparison Pages:** The large comparison table will be removed from high-traffic pages to boost performance. A full removal is preferred over a toggle to avoid engineering overhead.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=211.0)
  - [**LLM Traffic:** A data discrepancy was resolved: GrowthX-created content is growing, but overall site LLM traffic is declining. The team will now analyze all URLs to find growth opportunities.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
  - [**Renewal & Pilot:** The contract renewal is in validation. Strapi will pilot GrowthX's new self-serve product to reduce engineering bottlenecks.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=879.0)

## Topics

### Content Pipeline & Quality

  - [**Publishing Schedule:** Two articles were delayed by a code formatting bug (code escaping the formatter).](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)
      - [**Resolution:** The bug fix is in progress.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)
      - [**Impact:** Nine articles will be published this week instead of the usual seven.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)
  - [**Video Quality Control:** Incorrect, outdated V4 videos were embedded on the high-traffic WordPress and Contentful comparison pages.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=344.0)
      - [**Action:** Victor fixed the two pages.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=344.0)
      - [**Preventative Action:** GrowthX will audit all comparison pages this week to ensure videos are current and relevant.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=344.0)
      - [**Strategy:** Add videos (migration stories or V5 getting-started guides) to top-performing, text-heavy pages to improve digestibility.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=442.0)

### Website Issues & Performance

  - [**Integration Page Logos:** Logos are missing from the main landing page.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
      - [**Status:** The issue is a low priority due to a website bottleneck.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
  - [**Comparison Table Removal:** The large table at the top of comparison pages will be removed to boost performance.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=211.0)
      - [**Decision:** A full removal is preferred over a toggle button to avoid engineering overhead.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=254.0)
      - [**Fallback:** If removal is too complex, the table will be moved to the bottom of the page.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=309.0)
  - [**October Performance (YTD):**](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0)
      - [**Pace:** On track to match or outpace September for non-branded impressions and conversion events.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0)
      - [**Top Performers:** VS Code Extensions and Next.js for SEO articles.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=541.0)
      - [**Hypothesis:** The May conversion peak was an unintended side effect of a massive, temporary increase in non-branded search traffic from bad forum links.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=584.0)

### LLM Traffic Analysis

  - [**Data Discrepancy:** A conflict between Victor's and Vivek's LLM traffic data was resolved.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
      - [**Cause:** Vivek's chart was filtered to show only GrowthX-created URLs, which are growing.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
      - [**Clarification:** Overall site LLM traffic is declining, which is a concern as it should be growing with general LLM usage.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=763.0)
  - [**New Strategy:** The team will now analyze LLM traffic across *all* URLs to identify growth opportunities.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=862.0)

### Renewal & Product Pilot

  - [**Contract Renewal:** The renewal contract is in validation on Strapi's end.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=879.0)
  - [**Product Pilot:** Strapi will pilot GrowthX's new self-serve product.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=907.0)
      - [**Rationale:** To reduce engineering bottlenecks and enable the Strapi team to self-serve content updates.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=907.0)
      - [**Status:** GrowthX is aware of Strapi's interest and will provide a timeline for the pilot.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=907.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)
      - [Fix the code formatting bug to enable publishing the two delayed articles.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)
      - [Audit all comparison pages to ensure videos are current and relevant.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=344.0)
      - [Analyze LLM traffic across all URLs to find growth opportunities.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=862.0)
  - [**Strapi:**](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=211.0)
      - [Remove the large comparison table from high-traffic pages (fallback: move to bottom).](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=211.0)
      - [Finalize validation of the renewal contract.](https://fathom.video/share/RhqXVTssQgnQ24uJh8LeVQBRxjBcNXTL?tab=summary&timestamp=879.0)

action_items.description: Remove comparison table from published Strapi vs pages; if blocked, move to bottom
Audit comparison pages; update/add videos; share recs w/ Paul & Theo; implement
Update Looker LLM referral report to include all URLs
